                              CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                     Big Data Analytics


• How does Netflix know what you will watch next?




                    CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                         Big Data Analytics

• How does Netflix know what you will watch next!


       • What you watch, pause, skip
       • What millions of similar users watch
       • Content features (genre, actors, duration)




       • Massive user behavior data
       • Distributed storage & processing
       • Machine learning at scale




                        CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                      Big Data Analytics


• How does Google Maps predict traffic?




                     CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                        Big Data Analytics


• How does Google Maps predict traffic?

       • Live GPS data from millions of phones
       • Historical traffic patterns
       • Real-time events (accidents, weather)




       • Data arrives every second
       • Routes updated in near real time
       • Must handle failures gracefully




                       CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                          Big Data Analytics


• How does X/Twitter/Instagram/Tiktok know what’s
  trending right now?

• Millions of posts per minute
• Hashtags, keywords, mentions
• User location and time




• High velocity data streams
• Sudden spikes (viral events)
• Noise, spam, and bots



                         CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                      Big Data Analytics


YouTube Content Moderation
  • Flagging inappropriate videos at upload scale


InDrive/Cream (ride-hailing surge pricing)
  • Real-time demand-supply analytics


Amazon (E-Comm. Recommendations)
  • Clickstreams + purchases at scale



                     CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                    Big Data Analytics




Will Excel / Traditional DBs be able to deal with it?




                   CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                 Why would Excel fails?



• Memory limitations

• Single-machine processing

• No concurrency

• No streaming

• No fault tolerance




                   CS-895 Big Data Analytics
       Why would Traditional RDBMS fails?



• Structured schema only

• Vertical scaling

• Batch-oriented processing

• High write volume issues

• Fault tolerance




                     CS-895 Big Data Analytics
           Why Traditional Systems Fail?



• Single-machine processing



• RDBMS scaling issues


• ACID vs Scalability


• Network & disk bottlenecks

                  CS-895 Big Data Analytics
         1. Single-Machine Processing?



Limitations of one machine:

      • CPU, RAM, and disk are finite


      • Cannot hold TBs of data in memory


      • Failure = total downtime



                CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                  2. RDBMS Scaling!!!



Scaling
             Description        Pros           Cons
Type
             Bigger             Easy to        Expensive,
Vertical
             CPU/RAM            upgrade        hits ceiling
                                               Complex,
             Add                Scales         needs
Horizontal
             machines           indefinitely   distributed
                                               logic



                   CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                3. ACID vs Scalability



• ACID guarantees: Atomicity, Consistency,
  Isolation, Durability
• High volume/ high velocity  hard to
  maintain full ACID
• Big Data Systems often sacrifice strict
  consistency for speed and availability




                 CS-895 Big Data Analytics
          4. Network & Disk Bottlenecks


Why network & disk matter:


• Reading 1 TB from disk at 100 MB/s 
  bottleneck
• Moving data over network is slower than
  computation
• I/O dominates performance in Big Data




                CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                    What is Big Data?




Big Data refers to datasets that are too large, too
fast, or too complex for traditional data processing
systems to capture, store, manage, or analyze
efficiently.




                   CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                   What is Big Data?




Big Data = Data that breaks traditional systems
in volume, velocity, or variety, and requires
new tools and approaches to generate value.




                  CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                    Characteristics of Big Data


                        Five V’s of Spatial Big Data


    Volume           Variety          Velocity         Veracity           Value


                    Different forms     Analysis of
                        of data       Streaming Data                    Information for
                                                       Uncertainty of
    Scale of Data                                                          Decision
                                                          Data
                     Diversity of         Speed                             Making
                      sources




1
                         V1 = Volume


• The amount of data is too large to store or
  process on single machine

• Single-machine processing fails → data
  doesn’t fit in memory or disk
• Example: 1 TB read at 100 MB/s takes ~2.8
  hours

• Netflix user activity, Twitter firehose, Bank
  transaction logs


                  CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                       V2 = Velocity


• The speed at which new data is generated and
  must be processed.


• ACID constraints + single server → cannot
  process millions of events per second
• Traditional DBs = batch-oriented, high



• Ex. Google Maps traffic updates every second


                 CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                        V3 = Variety


• Data comes in multiple forms: structured,
  semi-structured, and unstructured

• RDBMS expect structured data (tables +
  schema)
• Big Data sources: logs, images, videos, JSON,
  sensor data

• Ex. Tweets + hashtags + images + geolocation
• Ex. Netflix watch history + ratings + metadata


                 CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                       V4 = Veracity



• Data is often messy, incomplete, or uncertain


• Single-machine or DB systems assume “clean”
  data
• Handling noise, duplicates, or missing values
  at scale is difficult

• Social media spam, Inaccurate GPS readings,
  Sensor errors in IoT data


                 CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                         V5 = Value


• The usefulness of data for decision-making


• Collecting massive data is useless if it cannot
  be processed and analyzed efficiently
• Traditional systems fail to extract insights
  quickly from huge, fast, or messy data

• Netflix recommendations increasing
  engagement
• Fraud alerts preventing losses
• Trend detectionCS-895
                  for Big Data
                        marketing
                               Analytics campaigns
                        Comparative Analysis

          What it           Traditional
   V                                          Example
          Means             System Limitation
                                                     Netflix 1 TB
Volume Size too big         Single-machine fails
                                                     logs
                                                     Google Maps
Velocity Too fast           Batch DB too slow
                                                     traffic
          Many data
Variety                     RDBMS rigid schema Twitter posts
          types
                            Hard to clean &          Social media
Veracity Messy / noisy
                            process                  spam
          Insights at                                Fraud
Value                       Too slow / manual
          scale                                      detection

                         CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                  Big Data Philosophy


• Distributed Computing

• Data Locality

• Fault Tolerance

• Commodity Hardware


• Trade-offs: Consistency vs Scalability



                    CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                           At A Glance


Principle                               Intuition
Distributed       Many machines share the work → speed
computing         + redundancy
                  Move compute to data → reduce
Data locality
                  network overhead
                  Expect failure → design for automatic
Fault tolerance
                  recovery
Commodity         Cheap, replaceable → scale
hardware          inexpensively
                  Consistency vs scalability → prioritize
Trade-offs
                  what matters

                   CS-895 Big Data Analytics
            Big Data Engineering vs Analytics




Big Data Engineering:                Big Data Analytics:
• Build and maintain                 • Extract insights from
  the infrastructure                   data
• Collect, store, and                • Build models and
  prepare data                         predictions
• Ensure scalability,                • Support decision-
  reliability, fault                   making
  tolerance



                  CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                       Types of Analytics


Type           Question                  Example
                                         Daily active users, total
Descriptive    What happened?
                                         sales last month
               Why did it                Drop in users due to
Diagnostic
               happen?                   feature change
               What will
Predictive                               Next month’s sales forecast
               happen?
                                         Suggest promotion
               What should we
Prescriptive                             strategies to maximize
               do?
                                         sales


                      CS-895 Big Data Analytics
               Simple Big Data Analytics Pipeline


• Logs: Raw data generated by users, devices, or systems
• Storage: Persist the data using distributed storage
  (HDFS, S3)
• Processing: Clean, filter, and aggregate the data
  (Spark/Hadoop)
• Feature Extraction: Convert raw data into meaningful
  inputs for models
• Model: Apply analytics or machine learning algorithms
• Decision: Generate actionable insights or automate
  responses

                     CS-895 Big Data Analytics
          Simple Big Data Analytics Pipeline (Netflix)



• Logs: what you watched

• Storage: data lake

• Processing: compute watch frequency, genre stats

• Features: user-genre vectors

• Model: collaborative filtering

• Decision: suggest next movie




                       CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                                       Course Goal

• This course contributes to the development of the following
capabilities:
    1. To learn about basic concepts, technical challenges, and opportunities in
      big data analytics (including the big picture).
    2. To learn about common algorithmic and statistical techniques used to
      perform big data analysis.
    3. To learn and get hands-on experience analyzing large datasets using
      Hadoop, Apache Spark, Python, and NoSQL.
    4. To learn about different types of scenarios and applications in big data
      analytics.


                               CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                 Course Contents (evolving)

• Introduction to Big Data Analytics (Big Picture)

Part 1
• Big Data Analytics: Frequent Itemsets Mining
• Big Data Analytics: Finding Similar Items
• Big Data Analytics: Stream Data Processing
• Big Data Analytics: Graph Processing
• Big Data Analytics: Recommender Systems
• Big Data Analytics: Dimensionality Reduction
Part 2 …
Part 3 …

                      CS-895 Big Data Analytics
               Course Contents (evolving)

Part 1 …
Part 2
• Big Data Technology: Hadoop (Map-Reduce Model)
• Big Data Technology: Apache Spark (in-memory analytics)
• Big Data Technology: Apache Spark (ML Lib.)
• Big Data Technology: NoSQL/Cassandra/MongoDB

Part 3
• Big Data Applications: Retrieval Augmented Generation
• Big Data Applications: Text Analytics
• Big Data Applications for Healthcare/Business

• Classroom lectures
• Hands-on sessions / Drill Lessons



Text Book:

1. Mining of Massive Datasets by Jure
   Leskovec Anand Rajaraman and Jeffrey
   Ullman.
               (Part – I)

    https://web.stanford.edu/class/cs246/


                         CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                              Teaching method

Reference Books:

• Data Science for Business: What you need to know about data
  mining and data-analytic thinking by Provost, F., & Fawcett, T.

• Big data analytics: from strategic planning to enterprise integration
  with tools, techniques, NoSQL, and graph. Elsevier by Loshin, D.

• Data Mining Concepts and Techniques - Jiawei han & Micheline
  Kamber Harcourt.

• Learning Spark: Lightning-Fast Data Analytics by Jules S. Damji,
  Brooke Wenig, Tathagata Das, Denny Lee.

                                           machine is being
